subject: Astronomy & Space
title: Novae: White Dwarf Explosions
description: One-minute skill: Novae: White Dwarf Explosions.

hook.text: A nova occurs when a white dwarf steals gas from a companion star until the surface ignites in a thermonuclear explosion.

buildup.text: Unlike supernovae, novae don't destroy the star. The white dwarf survives and can explode again—some recur every few decades.

discovery.text: T Coronae Borealis is a recurrent nova that erupts roughly every 80 years. Its next eruption is expected soon.

twist.text: During a nova, the star brightens by 10,000 times in days. Ancient astronomers recorded them as 'new stars' appearing in the sky.

climax.text: Nova explosions eject enriched gas into space—contributing elements like lithium and nitrogen to the galaxy.

punchline.text: Stolen gas ignites on a dead star's surface. Not dead enough to stop exploding.

quiz.question: How do novae differ from supernovae?
options: The white dwarf survives and can erupt again, unlike a supernova which destroys the star
Novae are caused by black holes while supernovae are caused by neutron stars
Novae only occur in galaxies far from the Milky Way
